series_metadata.series_description: In accordance with sections 4765.101, 4765.11, 4765.15, 4765.17, 4766.08, and 4766.11 of the Ohio Revised Code (ORC), the State Board of Emergency Medical, Fire, and Transportation Services (via the Division of EMS) may investigate and take disciplinary action against certificate-holder, license-holder, permit-holder, charter-holder, EMS certificate of accreditation-holder, or EMS certificate of approval-holder who violates any section of ORC or Ohio Administrative Code pertaining to their certificate , license, permit, charter, certificate of accreditation, or certificate of approval. The case file will include all documents gathered as a part of the investigation into alleged violations including: findings, information regarding hearings, recommended disciplinary actions and appeals; dispositions; adjudications; and consent agreements. Limited information regarding case (such as name, case number, final action) is maintained in electronic databases.

retention_rules.disposition: Electronic: Active cases (cases that are not closed) resulting in discipline that may include indefinite suspension, revocation, or permanent revocation, will be retained permanently per advice of legal counsel. ; Paper: Active cases (cases that are not closed) resulting in discipline that may include indefinite suspension, revocation, or permanent revocation, will be retained permanently per advice of legal counsel., Electronic: Permanent ; Paper: Permanent
